title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is for sale for $12.5 million by retiring owners Evan and Maria Marley.
The sale includes the restaurant's commercial space at 11 West Creek Road and staff housing on Nobadeer Farm Road.
Current owners plan to ensure a smooth transition with Evan Marley temporarily staying on after the sale.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's national soccer team left Boston after over a month at the Four Seasons Hotel since early June.
Les Bleus shared a farewell video with hotel staff and noted staying at the hotel with personalized touches.
France will compete for third place in Miami against England after losing to Spain 2-0 in the semifinals.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon's production company, Artists Equity, is developing a movie about Karen Read's controversial murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges in 2022 regarding the death of her boyfriend, Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe.
Multiple adaptations of Read's case are underway, including projects by Lifetime, Prime Video, and Netflix.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve Carell celebrated his wife Nancy's 60th birthday at Tresca in Boston's North End.
Nancy Carell, née Walls, is a Cohasset native and Boston College graduate who owns a store in Marshfield.
Steve and Nancy Carell, who met at Second City Theater, married in 1995 and shared screen time in multiple projects.

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Residents in Saugus, Massachusetts, face extremely high water bills, with one bill nearly reaching $200,000.
The town of Saugus is reviewing about a dozen complaints possibly linked to new water meters and monitoring systems.
Town officials are exploring solutions as affected residents face penalties, liens, and financial strain from the bills.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at the Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16, captivating fans with his newer material.
At 85, Dylan delivered a clear, distinct performance featuring 16 songs, including recent work and unexpected arrangements.
Openers Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan impressed, with Spencer covering Dylan's 'To Make You Feel My Love'.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces middle seats turned into tables on new Airbus A321XLR aircraft in Economy Plus.
The table provides extra space from armrest to armrest, with a leather-like cover and cup holders.
United will start domestic flights with the A321XLR in fall 2026, with international flights by early 2027.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The 'Gilmore Girls' pilot house at 31 Victoria Ave., Markham, Ontario, is for sale at $2.76 million USD.
The iconic home, built around 1875 and modernized with additions, features four bedrooms and four bathrooms.
The 550-square-foot barn on the property, originally a bakery, is zoned for commercial use and retains historical features.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Gary Newell, a longtime youth soccer coach from Maine, was honored as Boston's 'Unsung Hero' by FIFA at the 2026 World Cup.
Newell was recognized for his decades of contributions to youth soccer and received a surprise video message from England's Jude Bellingham.
The award presentation included video tributes from former players, highlighting Newell's impact on youth soccer development.

title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in Boston Public Garden, having surpassed its typical 30-50 year lifespan.
Routine inspections by Friends of the Public Garden had found the tree structurally sound, with no injuries reported in the fall.
Weeping willows have been part of the Garden since early 1900s; regular tree upkeep includes pruning and disease management.
